steps.title: Identify the key logical structure
steps.content: The passage presents a seeming contradiction: Panama City residents use parks more (53.5% vs. 35.7%), yet they live closer to parks less often than Fortaleza residents. The final sentence explicitly draws a conclusion from this contradiction: “greater proximity alone can't explain the difference in park use.” The word “alone” is decisive—it tells you that something beyond proximity must account for the difference.

steps.title: Match the conclusion to the choices
steps.content: Choice D states exactly this point: the difference “is caused by something other than the parks' proximity to the residents.” The other three introduce ideas nowhere in the text—researcher expectations, survey errors, or outdated sources. That matches choice D — D is correct.
